title: clematis terniflora var. robusta
intro: Clematis terniflora var. robusta, also known as sweet autumn clematis, is a deciduous vine native to East Asia, characterized by its clusters of small, white, fragrant flowers that bloom in late summer and early fall. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of sweet autumn clematis include its potential to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce stress and anxiety, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional Chinese medicine. In herbal preparations, sweet autumn clematis is often dried and used as a tea, or made into a tincture or infusion to harness its medicinal properties.
